summary: The /neuralrelay history command is well-designed and covers the core use case cleanly. Two issues need fixing before merge: the off-by-one bug in directory listing (medium — silently drops a run) and the missing JSON error handling (low — crashes on corrupted files). Both fixes are straightforward. The handoff chain for this task was excellent — the dev agent's honest coverage gaps directly led to the test agent finding the corrupted-JSON bug.

issues.description: Off-by-one in chain directory listing: 'ls -r | head -5' includes the ls header line, causing only 4 of 5 chains to display when exactly 5 exist.
issues.resolution: Use 'ls -1r | head -5' or 'find ... -type d | sort -r | head -5'. The find approach is more portable and avoids ls parsing issues entirely. Prefer find.

issues.description: Corrupted 04-review-agent.json crashes the entire history command instead of showing 'incomplete' for that single chain.
issues.resolution: Add error handling around JSON parsing: if a handoff file exists but fails to parse, treat it as missing and display 'error' status for that chain. Never let one bad chain break the entire output.

issues.description: The command definition doesn't specify the output format for the 'incomplete' status case — should it show dashes for missing values or omit the columns?
issues.resolution: Standardize: show the chain ID and task summary (from 01-ba-agent.json if available), and use '—' for verdict, quality score, and total tokens when 04-review-agent.json is missing.

recommendations: The dev agent's coverage gap about corrupted JSON directly enabled the test agent to find the error-handling bug — this validates that honest coverage_gaps are the highest-value field in the protocol
For future command additions, include a standard 'error handling' section in the command markdown template specifying how each command should behave with corrupted or missing data
The test agent correctly prioritized coverage gaps over property verification — the off-by-one was found through property falsification (priority 2), showing both priority levels are productive
Consider adding a 'commands must handle corrupted input gracefully' rule to CLAUDE.md quality standards to prevent this class of bug across all commands
